acceptance.assertion: Periodic worktree hygiene is integrated into an existing runtime-retention or convergence producer; no duplicate cleanup daemon or second lifecycle truth model is introduced.

Automatic reconciliation is bounded per run and archives only clean terminal checkouts proven by exact merged PR head and default-branch evidence, while dirty, ambiguous, coordinated and foreign-owned worktrees remain fail-closed.
Automatic cleanup never bypasses active retention, recovery refs, the 24-hour archive grace, or the persisted dry-run plan and plan_sha256 precondition.
T082 is completed successfully before periodic mutating effects are enabled.
Tests cover periodic idempotence and safety gates, and live readback demonstrates convergence without deleting protected or active worktrees.

approval.note: Implement scheduling in existing retention/convergence paths; do not introduce an independent competing cleanup daemon.

goal: Run bounded worktree lifecycle reconciliation automatically through the existing retention/convergence machinery, avoiding a second cleanup daemon while preserving recovery-first archive, active-retention protection, the 24-hour grace period and typed dry-run/hash-gated cleanup.

metadata.live_basis: The deployed grip exists and is live, but PR #370 intentionally added no periodic producer scheduling. Final reviewed PR diff SHA-256: 01cea5fbef3fc3d5c5ef4cc96b09f8f74dd2bce2b0ad95af7edba19f6064c4d0.

title: Integrate worktree hygiene into runtime retention convergence
